title: fix: remove legacy deploy workflow name
author: ElliotEtag
pr_body_excerpt:
- Removes the temporary `Deploy gen.pollinations.ai` compatibility entry.
- Keeps API-reference regeneration listening only to `Deploy / gen.pollinations.ai`.
- Follows successful production deploy and dependent docs-workflow verification.
- Validated with `actionlint` and `git diff --check`.

gist.headline: One deploy name, fewer ghosts
gist.blurb: The docs hive has stopped listening for a temporary deploy alias. One canonical signal now wakes the API-reference gardener.
gist.summary: The API-reference regeneration workflow now listens only for the canonical `Deploy / gen.pollinations.ai` workflow name, removing the temporary `Deploy gen.pollinations.ai` compatibility entry.
gist.impact: Deploy-triggered API documentation regeneration now follows one verified production workflow signal, reducing duplicate legacy configuration.
gist.visual_concept: A cozy garden shed automation scene: Polly, wearing a tiny maintainer cap, removes an old handwritten alias tag from a single glowing deployment bell while a code-plant API reference blooms beside it. The scene represents retiring compatibility wiring while preserving the canonical docs regeneration trigger.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene in a warm garden-shed hackerspace where Polly the cute yellow-and-brown striped bee mascot wears a tiny maintainer cap and gently removes an old label from a glowing lime green (#ecf874) deployment bell marked in simple pixel text `Deploy / gen.pollinations.ai`; beside her, an API-reference code-plant blooms from a pastel pot. Cozy 8-bit pixel art aesthetic, soft pastel gradients, warm lighting, retro gaming vibes, subtle CRT glow, dark purple (#110518) outlines, nostalgic and emotionally warm.
